
# Global Carbon Cycle

$$
c_1\frac{dT_1}{dt} = \lambda T_1 + \kappa(T_2 - T_1) + Q_2 \ln\frac{C_A}{C^*_A}
$$
$$
c_2\frac{dT_2}{dt} = -\kappa(T_2 - T_1)
$$

$$
\frac{dC_L}{dt} = \Pi C_A - \Pi C_A^*\frac{C_L}{C_L^*} \exp(\alpha \beta T_1)
$$

$$
\frac{dC_1}{dt} = \nu_1 \left( C_A - \epsilon(C_1) \right) - \nu_2 \left( C_1 - \frac{V_1}{V_2} C_2 \right)
$$
$$
\frac{dC_2}{dt} = \nu_2 \left( C_1 - \frac{V1}{V2} C_2 \right)
$$

$$
\Pi(C_A) = \Pi C_A^*
$$
* $c_1$ upper box heat capacity ($c_1$ = 7.7 W a m$^{-2}$ K$^{-1}$)
* $c_1$ lower box heat capacity ($c_2$ = 100 W a m$^{-2}$ K$^{-1}$)
* $T_1$ is the globally averaged temperature anomaly of the atmosphere and upper ocean in K
* $T_2$ is the globally averaged temperature anomaly of the deep ocean in K
* $\lambda$ is the climate feedback parameter
* $\kappa$ is a heat transfer coefficient ($\kappa$ = 0.57 W m$^{-2}$ K$^{-1}$)
* $Q_2$ Radiative forcing due to doubled CO2 (3.7 W m$^{-2}$) (Dessler: uses 5.35 W m$^{-2}$)
* $C_A$ is the atmospheric carbon in ppmv
* $C^*_A$ is the PI atmospheric carbon in ppmv ($C^*_A = 286$ ppmv)
* $V_2/V_1$ is the ocean box volume ratio ($V_2/V_1 = 50$)
